rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in component type scopes when describing signal flow between ports.
Use visible identifiers from the current component scope to define variables and expressions in AGREE contracts.
generic_rules: AGREE annex blocks must be scoped to their owning component declaration.
Component type contracts should relate visible inputs to visible outputs without referencing implementation-only subcomponent paths.
Assignments in AGREE are valid only in implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents and connections.
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless explicit typed ports or constants are available.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees follow the syntax: `assume "label": expression;` and `guarantee "label": expression;`.
anti_patterns: Do not reference subcomponent ports in a component type annex unless represented through valid variables or outputs.
Do not invent new identifiers in AGREE clauses; use only visible identifiers from the current component scope.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and their mapping to AGREE variables
Model fusion and component implementation binding strategies
